[[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

[[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

{{commons category|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

{{commons category|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nantan, Kyoto]]

Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nantan, Kyoto]]

{{commons category|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nantan, Kyoto]]

{{commons category|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]]

Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]]

{{commons category|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:多治神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430844}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Taji Shrine |画像 = [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|58.42|N|135|31|12.06|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Taji Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era ({{ill|lt=704|Q23503|WD=Q23503|1=704}}-{{ill|lt=707|Q23510|WD=Q23510|1=AD 707}}) |本殿 = Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday in mid-October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}})
Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Taji Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Taji Shrine''' (たじじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hiyoshi-cho, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The two principal deities are: * '''{{ill|lt=Oyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, during the {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} era (704-707), by Tahara Sadaijin ({{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}}, also written Shiki no Miko), third imperial son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}According to ''{{ill|lt=Ruiju Sandai Kyaku|Q11665185|WD=Q11665185|1=Ruijū Sandai Kyaku}}''. ''Shoku Nihongi'' entry for Reiki 2, 8th month, 11th day says seventh prince.. In the mid-Heian ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as "Tanuma Shrine" in Funai District, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Because the settlement name at the shrine site was "Tahara-go," it is thought that the characters "Taji" came to be used, but a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1302|Q5612|WD=Q5612|1=1302}} ({{ill|lt=Kengen (Japan)|Q1327472|WD=Q1327472|lt=Kengen|1=Kengen}} 1) in the Kamakura period reads "Tanuma Daimyojin," suggesting there was also a period when it was called "Tanuma"''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,'' section on Taji Shrine in Funai District.. In Meiji 10, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precincts === File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|Main hall
Rebuilt four times; current hall was built in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|WD=Q7700|1=1775}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 5) by shrine carpenters from Banshu. Two-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. Registered cultural property of Kyoto Prefecture. File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) emaden.JPG|Ema hall File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === [[File:Taji-jinja (Nantan) setsumatsusha.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Precinct shrines]] * Inari Shrine * Tenmangu * Hachimangu * Ichiki Shrine === Main festivals === * {{ill|lt=Tahara no Onda|Q11576232|WD=Q11576232|1=Tahara no Onda}} ({{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1306|Q5716|WD=Q5716|1=1306}} ({{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, as a rite praying for a good harvest[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival1.html#tawaranoonta Tahara no Onda] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/tawaraonda.html Tahara no Onda] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. * Tahara no Kakkosuri (mid-October) :: Began in {{ill|lt=1414|Q6179|WD=Q6179|1=1414}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 21), early {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, as a thanksgiving rite for harvest. Formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}}[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/festival3.html#kakkosuri Tahara no Kakkosuri] (Nantan Guide), [https://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/kakkosuri.html Kakkosuri] (Kuchitanba Shrines).. === Cultural properties === See [http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/index.php?action=pages_view_mobile&page_id=195&room_id=0&nc_session10=rvufg5qse4mrj5m59igh1q4dd3 "Cultural Properties in Kyoto Prefecture" (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division)]. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national) === * Tahara no Onda - designated in 2000 (Heisei 12) === Kyoto Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Tahara no Kakkosuri - designated in 1983 (Showa 58) === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property === * Main hall (with 5 ridge tags) - registered in 1985 (Showa 60) === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environment Preservation District === * Taji Shrine Cultural Property Environment Preservation District - decided in 1985 (Showa 60) === Nantan City Designated Cultural Property === * Two mikoshi : Made in {{ill|lt=1845|Q7636|WD=Q7636|1=1845}} ({{ill|lt=Koka|Q1325317|WD=Q1325317|1=Kōka}} 2), late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, by Kyoto carpenter Yoshinoya Jinnojō. Designated in 1987 (Showa 62). === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}Hiyoshi-cho Tahara Miyago 2-2 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)|Q871551|WD=Q871551|lt=Hiyoshi Station|1=Hiyoshi Station (Kyoto)}}; about 15 minutes by Nantan municipal bus or by car ; Nearby * Kotaji Shrine (Tahara, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - Kakkosuri is dedicated in front of Kotaji Shrine during the Kakkosuri event[https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/shrine/hiyoshi/03.html#h030 Kotaji Shrine] (Tanba shrines). * Taji Shrine (Ikehata, Hiyoshi-cho, Nantan) - enshrined by division from this shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{small|(References used in writing this article)}} * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), section on Taji Shrine, Funai District * Local information board === Related literature === {{small|(Related literature not used directly in writing this article)}} {{Commons|Category:Taji-jinja (Nantan)}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1922|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976610/64}} Taji Shrine]|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Scenic Places Survey Report, Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collection. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taji Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]]
